REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF FOLSOM STREET HOTEL LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT FOLSOM STREET HOTEL LLC AND CROSS DEFENDANT SAM PATELS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - POS ATTACHED IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. NOTICE AND SEPARATE STATEMENT FAIL TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION IS UNCLEAR AS TO WHETHER THE MOTION IS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE COMPLAINT, CROSS-COMPLAINT, OR BOTH. NO ISSUES TO BE ADJUDICATED (THAT WOULD DISPOSE OF AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENSE OR ISSUE OF DUTY) ARE CLEARLY STATED. THE "GROUNDS" FOR THE MOTION APPEAR TO COVER (1) ONE OF TWO CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT (GROUND 1: SEEKING JUDGMENT ON BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM AND REFERENCING LACK OF DEFENSE TO THIS CLAIM); AND (2) FIVE OF SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANT (CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS ARE NOT EXPRESSLY ADDRESSED IN THE NOTICE OR POINTS AND AUTHORITIES). ADDITIONALLY, MOST OF THE "GROUNDS" FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION EXPRESSLY STATE THAT ONLY "SOME" OR "MANY" (AS OPPOSED TO ALL) OF THE CLAIMS FAIL FOR ONE REASON OR ANOTHER, E.G. "ELFAR WAIVED MANY OF HIS CLAIMS," "SOME OF ELFAR'S CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED." THEREFORE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT CANNOT BE GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Plaintiff And Cross Defendant Folsom Street Hotel Llc And Cross Defendant Sam Patels Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication - Pos Attached
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Cassady Toles, 415.474.1900 for Defendants/Opposing parties Elfar Joseph and Soma Corporation.
Howard Olsen, 415.931.4207 for Plaintiff/Moving party Folsom Street Hotel LLC.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF FOLSOM STREET HOTEL LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Plaintiff And Cross Defendant Folsom Street Hotel Llc And Cross Defendant Sam Patels Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication - Pos Attached is DENIED. Motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. Notice and Separate Statement fail to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. The Court notes that Plaintiff's Notice of Motion is unclear as to whether the motion is for summary judgment on the complaint, cross-complaint, or both. No issues to be adjudicated (that would dispose of an entire cause of action, defense or issue of duty) are clearly stated. The "grounds" for the motion appear to cover (1) one of two causes of action alleged in the Complaint (ground 1: seeking judgment on breach of contract claim and referencing lack of defense to this claim); and (2) five of seven causes of action alleged in the cross-complaint against the moving defendant (causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and intentional interference with contractual relations are not expressly addressed in the Notice or Points and Authorities). Additionally, most of the "grounds" for summary judgment/adjudication expressly state that only "some" or "many" (as opposed to all) of the claims fail for one reason or another, e.g. "Elfar waived many of his claims," "some of Elfar's claims are time-barred." Therefore, Motion for Summary Judgement cannot be granted. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
